title: Pungent, Velvety Chinese Pinto Beans Hunan Lo Mein
description: A savory, velvety Hunan-inspired lo mein dish featuring pinto beans, chicken, and a blend of spices, all stir-fried to perfection with a wok hei flavor.
instructions: Cook the lo mein noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
In a large skillet or wok, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Add the garlic, ginger, and scallions to the skillet and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the pinto beans, bean sprouts, and Hunan sauce to the skillet and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes.
Return the chicken to the skillet and stir-fry for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Add the cooked lo mein noodles to the skillet and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes, until everything is well combined.
Season with soy sauce and sugar to taste.
Transfer the lo mein to a serving platter and drizzle with sesame oil.
Garnish with additional scallions and sesame oil, if desired.
